preview_text:
The model rates Australia as the favorite with a 44% chance of victory, while Paraguay has a 26% path to a win. The underdog's probability reflects a meaningful but not insurmountable gap, positioning this as a contest where the model leans toward the higher-rated side but acknowledges genuine upset potential.

The draw emerges as a live outcome at 31%, suggesting competitive equilibrium between the teams. The spread across all three outcomes is relatively balanced—no single result dominates—which indicates a close match where either a Paraguay upset, a stalemate, or an Australian win all carry non-trivial probability.

The model and market diverge substantially here. Sportsbooks and prediction markets both assign Paraguay roughly 33–34% to win and Australia only 24–25%, inverting the model's ordering and widening Australia's implied edge to 9–10 percentage points. Both markets converge on a draw near 42–43%, well above the model's 31%. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; users should weigh the divergence as material uncertainty in this fixture.
